steps.title: Set the circle area equal to the square area
steps.content: Set the circle area equal to the square area. Find the circle's area from $A=\pi r^2$ with $r=3$, then set that equal to the square's area $s^2$.

steps.title: Find the circle's area
steps.content:
Use the circle area formula $A = \pi r^2$ with radius $r = 3$:
$$A_{\text{circle}} = \pi(3)^2 = 9\pi$$

steps.title: Set up and solve for the square's side
steps.content:
The square has the same area as the circle, so if $s$ is the side length:
$$s^2 = 9\pi$$
Taking the square root of both sides:
$$s = \sqrt{9\pi} = \sqrt{9} \cdot \sqrt{\pi} = 3\sqrt{\pi}$$
That confirms C is correct.
